examples.title: Algorithm: MDM Device Management Operations
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating Mobile Device Management operations including device enrollment, policy enforcement, and remote wipe
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MDM_Operations
INPUT: device_id, operation_type, policy_config
OUTPUT: operation_status, device_state

BEGIN
    // Device Enrollment Procedure
    PROCEDURE Enroll_Device(device_id, user_credentials)
        IF Validate_Credentials(user_credentials) THEN
            device_record = CREATE_Device_Record(device_id)
            device_record.status = "enrolled"
            device_record.user_id = user_credentials.user_id
            device_record.enrollment_time = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            device_record.security_posture = "pending_assessment"
            
            // Generate device certificate
            certificate = Generate_Device_Certificate(device_id)
            Store_Certificate(device_id, certificate)
            
            RETURN "enrolled", device_record
        ELSE
            RETURN "enrollment_failed", NULL
        END IF
    END PROCEDURE
    
    // Policy Enforcement Procedure
    PROCEDURE Enforce_Policy(device_id, policy_config)
        device_record = GET_Device_Record(device_id)
        
        // Check device encryption status
        IF NOT device_record.encryption_enabled THEN
            RETURN "policy_violation", "encryption_required"
        END IF
        
        // Check OS version compliance
        IF NOT Check_OS_Version(device_record.os_version, 
                                policy_config.min_os_version) THEN
            RETURN "policy_violation", "os_update_required"
        END IF
        
        // Check screen lock enabled
        IF NOT device_record.screen_lock_enabled THEN
            RETURN "policy_violation", "screen_lock_required"
        END IF
        
        // Apply security policies
        APPLY_Security_Policies(device_id, policy_config)
        
        RETURN "policy_compliant", device_record
    END PROCEDURE
    
    // Remote Wipe Procedure
    PROCEDURE Remote_Wipe(device_id, wipe_type)
        device_record = GET_Device_Record(device_id)
        
        IF wipe_type = "full" THEN
            // Wipe all device data
            CLEAR_All_Data(device_id)
            CLEAR_Cloud_Cache(device_id)
            DELETE_Device_Certificate(device_id)
            device_record.status = "wiped"
        ELSE IF wipe_type = "selective" THEN
            // Wipe only corporate data (MAM selective wipe)
            CLEAR_Container_Data(device_id)
            CLEAR_Corporate_Apps(device_id)
            CLEAR_Cloud_Cache(device_id)
            device_record.corporate_data_wiped = TRUE
        END IF
        
        LOG_Audit_Event("remote_wipe", device_id, wipe_type)
        
        RETURN "wipe_completed", device_record
    END PROCEDURE
    
    // Device Health Check
    PROCEDURE Check_Device_Health(device_id)
        device_record = GET_Device_Record(device_id)
        
        health_score = 100
        
        IF NOT device_record.encryption_enabled THEN
            health_score = health_score - 30
        END IF
        
        IF NOT device_record.screen_lock_enabled THEN
            health_score = health_score - 20
        END IF
        
        IF device_record.jailbreak_detected THEN
            health_score = 0
        END IF
        
        IF device_record.malware_detected THEN
            health_score = 0
        END IF
        
        RETURN health_score
    END PROCEDURE

    // Main execution example
    result = Enroll_Device("DEVICE_001", user_creds)
    policy_result = Enforce_Policy("DEVICE_001", corp_policy)
    health = Check_Device_Health("DEVICE_001")
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates core MDM operations: device enrollment with certificate generation, policy enforcement checking encryption/OS/screen lock, remote wipe for full or selective (MAM) data erasure, and device health assessment. These are fundamental operations in mobile-cloud security management.

examples.title: Python: Zero Trust Authentication System
examples.description: Implementation of Zero Trust security model with continuous verification, MFA, and least privilege access for mobile-cloud security
examples.code:
import hashlib
import hmac
import time
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum

class DeviceTrustLevel(Enum):
    UNTRUSTED = 0
    LOW = 1
    MEDIUM = 2
    HIGH = 3
    FULLY_TRUSTED = 4

class ZeroTrustAuthenticator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.devices = {}
        self.users = {}
        self.access_logs = []
        self.trust_conditions = {
            'min_trust_level': DeviceTrustLevel.MEDIUM,
            'require_mfa': True,
            'require_device_cert': True,
            'max_failed_attempts': 3,
            'risk_threshold': 0.7
        }
    
    def register_device(self, device_id, user_id, device_info):
        """Register a new device in the Zero Trust system"""
        self.devices[device_id] = {
            'device_id': device_id,
            'user_id': user_id,
            'device_info': device_info,
            'certificate': self._generate_device_cert(device_id),
            'trust_level': DeviceTrustLevel.LOW,
            'last_verified': None,
            'security_posture': self._assess_security_posture(device_info),
            'is_jailbroken': device_info.get('is_jailbroken', False),
            'os_version': device_info.get('os_version', '0'),
            'encryption_enabled': device_info.get('encryption_enabled', False)
        }
        print(f"[+] Device {device_id} registered with trust level: LOW")
        return self.devices[device_id]
    
    def _generate_device_cert(self, device_id):
        """Generate a simple device certificate"""
        cert_data = f"CERT:{device_id}:{time.time()}"
        return hashlib.sha256(cert_data.encode()).hexdigest()
    
    def _assess_security_posture(self, device_info):
        """Assess device security posture based on multiple factors"""
        score = 0
        max_score = 100
        
        # Check OS version (simplified)
        if device_info.get('os_version', '0') >= '15.0':
            score += 25
        
        # Check encryption
        if device_info.get('encryption_enabled', False):
            score += 30
        
        # Check screen lock
        if device_info.get('screen_lock_enabled', False):
            score += 20
        
        # Check if device is jailbroken/rooted
        if device_info.get('is_jailbroken', False):
            return 0  # Critical security issue
        
        # Check antivirus/biometric
        if device_info.get('biometric_enabled', False):
            score += 15
        
        if device_info.get('av_enabled', False):
            score += 10
        
        return score
    
    def verify_device(self, device_id):
        """Continuous device verification - key Zero Trust principle"""
        if device_id not in self.devices:
            return False, "Device not registered"
        
        device = self.devices[device_id]
        
        # Check for jailbreak/root (critical fail)
        if device['is_jailbroken']:
            device['trust_level'] = DeviceTrustLevel.UNTRUSTED
            return False, "Jailbroken device not allowed"
        
        # Re-assess security posture
        device['security_posture'] = self._assess_security_posture(device['device_info'])
        device['last_verified'] = datetime.now()
        
        # Update trust level based on posture
        if device['security_posture'] >= 80:
            device['trust_level'] = DeviceTrustLevel.FULLY_TRUSTED
        elif device['security_posture'] >= 60:
            device['trust_level'] = DeviceTrustLevel.HIGH
        elif device['security_posture'] >= 40:
            device['trust_level'] = DeviceTrustLevel.MEDIUM
        else:
            device['trust_level'] = DeviceTrustLevel.LOW
        
        return True, device['trust_level']
    
    def authenticate_user(self, user_id, device_id, mfa_token=None):
        """Zero Trust authentication with multiple verification factors"""
        auth_result = {
            'authenticated': False,
            'access_level': 'none',
            'reason': ''
        }
        
        # Step 1: Verify device first (Zero Trust - verify device always)
        device_verified, device_status = self.verify_device(device_id)
        if not device_verified:
            auth_result['reason'] = f"Device verification failed: {device_status}"
            self._log_access(user_id, device_id, 'denied', auth_result['reason'])
            return auth_result
        
        # Step 2: Check trust level meets minimum
        if isinstance(device_status, DeviceTrustLevel):
            if device_status.value < self.trust_conditions['min_trust_level'].value:
                auth_result['reason'] = f"Device trust level {device_status.name} below required"
                self._log_access(user_id, device_id, 'denied', auth_result['reason'])
                return auth_result
        
        # Step 3: Require MFA (Zero Trust - never trust single factor)
        if self.trust_conditions['require_mfa']:
            if not mfa_token or not self._verify_mfa(user_id, mfa_token):
                auth_result['reason'] = "MFA verification failed"
                self._log_access(user_id, device_id, 'denied', auth_result['reason'])
                return auth_result
        
        # Step 4: Calculate access based on least privilege
        access_level = self._calculate_least_privilege(device_status, user_id)
        
        auth_result['authenticated'] = True
        auth_result['access_level'] = access_level
        auth_result['reason'] = 'Access granted with least privilege'
        
        self._log_access(user_id, device_id, 'granted', access_level)
        return auth_result
    
    def _verify_mfa(self, user_id, token):
        """Verify MFA token (simplified)"""
        # In production, this would validate against real MFA systems
        return len(token) >= 6  # Simplified check
    
    def _calculate_least_privilege(self, trust_level, user_id):
        """Grant minimum permissions required (Least Privilege Principle)"""
        trust_to_access = {
            DeviceTrustLevel.FULLY_TRUSTED: ['read', 'write', 'admin', 'api'],
            DeviceTrustLevel.HIGH: ['read', 'write', 'api'],
            DeviceTrustLevel.MEDIUM: ['read', 'write'],
            DeviceTrustLevel.LOW: ['read'],
            DeviceTrustLevel.UNTRUSTED: []
        }
        return trust_to_access.get(trust_level, [])
    
    def _log_access(self, user_id, device_id, result, details):
        """Log all access attempts for continuous monitoring"""
        log_entry = {
            'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'user_id': user_id,
            'device_id': device_id,
            'result': result,
            'details': details
        }
        self.access_logs.append(log_entry)
    
    def get_access_logs(self, user_id=None):
        """Retrieve access logs for monitoring"""
        if user_id:
            return [log for log in self.access_logs if log['user_id'] == user_id]
        return self.access_logs

# Demo usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    zta = ZeroTrustAuthenticator()
    
    # Register a compliant device
    device_info = {
        'os_version': '15.4',
        'encryption_enabled': True,
        'screen_lock_enabled': True,
        'biometric_enabled': True,
        'is_jailbroken': False
    }
    zta.register_device("DEVICE_001", "user123", device_info)
    
    # Try authentication with MFA
    print("\n--- Authentication Attempt ---")
    result = zta.authenticate_user("user123", "DEVICE_001", mfa_token="123456")
    print(f"Result: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}")
    
    # Register a non-compliant device
    compromised_device = {
        'os_version': '12.0',
        'encryption_enabled': False,
        'screen_lock_enabled': False,
        'is_jailbroken': True
    }
    zta.register_device("DEVICE_002", "user456", compromised_device)
    
    # Try authentication with compromised device
    print("\n--- Compromised Device Attempt ---")
    result = zta.authenticate_user("user456", "DEVICE_002", mfa_token="123456")
    print(f"Result: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates Zero Trust security principles: device verification before access, continuous authentication, MFA requirement, least privilege access based on device trust level, and audit logging. It shows how mobile devices are evaluated before granting cloud access.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for authentication checks, O(n) for log retrieval
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for device and log storage

examples.title: Java: MAM Containerization System
examples.description: Implementation of Mobile Application Management containerization for secure corporate data separation on BYOD devices
examples.explanation: This Java implementation demonstrates MAM containerization: creating isolated corporate containers on personal devices, applying security policies (encryption, biometric, screen capture blocking), detecting jailbreaks, enforcing credential validation with lockout, and selective corporate data wipe for offboarding scenarios.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for container operations, O(n) for device enrollment
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for container and device storage

examples.title: Python: Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB)
examples.description: Implementation of CASB functionality including shadow IT detection, DLP policies, threat protection, and access monitoring between mobile devices and cloud services
examples.code:
import json
import hashlib
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from collections import defaultdict

class ThreatLevel(Enum):
    SAFE = 0
    LOW = 1
    MEDIUM = 2
    HIGH = 3
    CRITICAL = 4

class CASB:
    """
    Cloud Access Security Broker - Security layer between mobile devices and cloud services
    Provides: Visibility, Compliance, Threat Protection, Data Security
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        # Discovered cloud services (shadow IT detection)
        self.discovered_services = {}
        
        # Approved cloud services
        self.approved_services = {
            'google': {'domain': 'google.com', 'category': 'productivity', 'risk_score': 10},
            'microsoft': {'domain': 'microsoft.com', 'category': 'productivity', 'risk_score': 10},
            'salesforce': {'domain': 'salesforce.com', 'category': 'crm', 'risk_score': 15},
            'aws': {'domain': 'amazonaws.com', 'category': 'infrastructure', 'risk_score': 20}
        }
        
        # DLP Policies
        self.dlp_policies = []
        self._init_dlp_policies()
        
        # Access logs
        self.access_logs = []
        
        # Threat detection patterns
        self.threat_patterns = {
            'data_exfiltration': ['upload', 'external', 'sync'],
            'anomalous_access': ['unusual_location', 'after_hours', 'new_device'],
            'malware_indicators': ['.exe', '.scr', '.bat', 'suspicious_download']
        }
        
        # Alert rules
        self.alert_rules = []
    
    def _init_dlp_policies(self):
        """Initialize Data Loss Prevention policies"""
        self.dlp_policies = [
            {
                'name': 'PCI_DSS_Compliance',
                'type': 'pattern_match',
                'pattern': r'\b\d{4}[-\s]?\d{4}[-\s]?\d{4}[-\s]?\d{4}\b',  # Credit card
                'action': 'block',
                'severity': 'high'
            },
            {
                'name': 'SSN_Protection',
                'type': 'pattern_match',
                'pattern': r'\b\d{3}[-\s]?\d{2}[-\s]?\d{4}\b',  # SSN
                'action': 'block',
                'severity': 'high'
            },
            {
                'name': 'Email_Encryption',
                'type': 'keyword_match',
                'keywords': ['confidential', 'proprietary', 'internal_only'],
                'action': 'encrypt',
                'severity': 'medium'
            },
            {
                'name': 'Source_Code_Protection',
                'type': 'extension_match',
                'extensions': ['.py', '.java', '.cpp', '.js', '.cs'],
                'action': 'block',
                'severity': 'high'
            }
        ]
    
    def discover_cloud_services(self, device_id, traffic_data):
        """
        Shadow IT Detection - Discover cloud services being used
        """
        discovered = []
        
        for entry in traffic_data:
            domain = entry.get('domain', '')
            service_name = self._identify_service(domain)
            
            if service_name and service_name not in self.discovered_services:
                risk_level = 'high' if service_name not in self.approved_services else 'low'
                
                service_info = {
                    'service': service_name,
                    'domain': domain,
                    'first_seen': datetime.now().isoformat(),
                    'risk_level': risk_level,
                    'category': self._categorize_service(service_name),
                    'device_count': 1
                }
                self.discovered_services[service_name] = service_info
                discovered.append(service_info)
                
                print(f"[!] Discovered cloud service: {service_name} ({risk_level} risk)")
            elif service_name in self.discovered_services:
                self.discovered_services[service_name]['device_count'] += 1
        
        return discovered
    
    def _identify_service(self, domain):
        """Identify cloud service from domain"""
        domain = domain.lower()
        for service, info in self.approved_services.items():
            if info['domain'] in domain:
                return service
        
        # Unknown service - potential shadow IT
        if domain:
            return f"unknown_{domain}"
        return None
    
    def _categorize_service(self, service_name):
        """Categorize service type"""
        categories = {
            'storage': ['dropbox', 'box', 'google_drive'],
            'productivity': ['google', 'microsoft', 'office'],
            'crm': ['salesforce', 'hubspot'],
            'social': ['facebook', 'twitter', 'linkedin'],
            'communication': ['slack', 'teams', 'zoom']
        }
        
        for category, services in categories.items():
            if any(s in service_name for s in services):
                return category
        return 'uncategorized'
    
    def inspect_data_transfer(self, device_id, data, direction='upload'):
        """
        DLP Inspection - Check data for sensitive content
        """
        results = {
            'violations': [],
            'actions_taken': [],
            'threat_level': ThreatLevel.SAFE
        }
        
        import re
        
        for policy in self.dlp_policies:
            violations = []
            
            if policy['type'] == 'pattern_match':
                pattern = re.compile(policy['pattern'])
                matches = pattern.findall(data)
                if matches:
                    violations.append({
                        'policy': policy['name'],
                        'matches': len(matches),
                        'action': policy['action']
                    })
            
            elif policy['type'] == 'keyword_match':
                for keyword in policy['keywords']:
                    if keyword.lower() in data.lower():
                        violations.append({
                            'policy': policy['name'],
                            'keyword': keyword,
                            'action': policy['action']
                        })
                        break
            
            elif policy['type'] == 'extension_match':
                for ext in policy['extensions']:
                    if ext in data.lower():
                        violations.append({
                            'policy': policy['name'],
                            'extension': ext,
                            'action': policy['action']
                        })
            
            if violations:
                for v in violations:
                    results['violations'].append(v)
                    
                    # Determine action
                    if v['action'] == 'block':
                        results['actions_taken'].append('BLOCKED')
                        results['threat_level'] = ThreatLevel.HIGH
                    elif v['action'] == 'encrypt':
                        results['actions_taken'].append('ENCRYPTED')
                        results['threat_level'] = max(results['threat_level'], ThreatLevel.MEDIUM)
        
        return results
    
    def evaluate_access_request(self, device_id, user_id, resource, context):
        """
        Evaluate and authorize access requests with threat detection
        """
        evaluation = {
            'allowed': True,
            'threat_level': ThreatLevel.SAFE,
            'risk_score': 0,
            'conditions': [],
            'actions': []
        }
        
        # Check device security posture
        if context.get('is_jailbroken', False):
            evaluation['allowed'] = False
            evaluation['threat_level'] = ThreatLevel.CRITICAL
            evaluation['risk_score'] = 100
            evaluation['conditions'].append('Jailbroken device detected')
            return evaluation
        
        # Check location risk
        location = context.get('location', 'unknown')
        if location == 'high_risk_country':
            evaluation['risk_score'] += 30
            evaluation['threat_level'] = ThreatLevel.MEDIUM
            evaluation['conditions'].append('High-risk location')
        
        # Check time-based anomaly
        access_hour = context.get('access_hour', 12)
        if access_hour < 6 or access_hour > 22:
            evaluation['risk_score'] += 20
            evaluation['conditions'].append('Unusual access time')
        
        # Check new device
        if context.get('new_device', True):
            evaluation['risk_score'] += 25
            evaluation['conditions'].append('First-time device')
        
        # Check for malware indicators
        user_agent = context.get('user_agent', '')
        for indicator in ['malware', 'exploit', 'hack']:
            if indicator in user_agent.lower():
                evaluation['allowed'] = False
                evaluation['threat_level'] = ThreatLevel.CRITICAL
                evaluation['risk_score'] = 100
                evaluation['conditions'].append('Malware indicator detected')
        
        # Risk threshold check
        if evaluation['risk_score'] > 70:
            evaluation['allowed'] = False
            evaluation['threat_level'] = ThreatLevel.HIGH
            evaluation['actions'].append('MFA_REQUIRED')
        elif evaluation['risk_score'] > 40:
            evaluation['actions'].append('MONITOR')
        
        # Log the access attempt
        self._log_access(device_id, user_id, resource, evaluation)
        
        return evaluation
    
    def _log_access(self, device_id, user_id, resource, evaluation):
        """Log all access attempts for audit"""
        log_entry = {
            'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'device_id': device_id,
            'user_id': user_id,
            'resource': resource,
            'allowed': evaluation['allowed'],
            'threat_level': evaluation['threat_level'].name,
            'risk_score': evaluation['risk_score'],
            'conditions': evaluation['conditions']
        }
        self.access_logs.append(log_entry)
    
    def generate_compliance_report(self):
        """Generate compliance and security report"""
        report = {
            'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'shadow_it_discovered': len([s for s in self.discovered_services.values() 
                                        if s['risk_level'] == 'high']),
            'total_services': len(self.discovered_services),
            'total_access_attempts': len(self.access_logs),
            'blocked_attempts': len([l for l in self.access_logs if not l['allowed']]),
            'high_risk_devices': len([l for l in self.access_logs 
                                    if l['threat_level'] in ['HIGH', 'CRITICAL']]),
            'services_by_risk': {}
        }
        
        # Count by risk level
        for service in self.discovered_services.values():
            risk = service['risk_level']
            report['services_by_risk'][risk] = report['services_by_risk'].get(risk, 0) + 1
        
        return report

# Demo
if __name__ == "__main__":
    casb = CASB()
    
    # Simulate traffic discovery (Shadow IT Detection)
    print("=== Shadow IT Discovery ===")
    traffic = [
        {'domain': 'drive.google.com', 'bytes': 50000},
        {'domain': 'dropbox.com', 'bytes': 100000},  # Shadow IT!
        {'domain': 'unknown-pirate-site.com', 'bytes': 2000}  # Shadow IT!
    ]
    casb.discover_cloud_services('DEVICE_001', traffic)
    
    # DLP Inspection
    print("\n=== DLP Data Inspection ===")
    test_data = "credit card: 4532-1234-5678-9010 and confidential data"
    dlp_result = casb.inspect_data_transfer('DEVICE_001', test_data, 'upload')
    print(f"DLP Result: {json.dumps(dlp_result, indent=2)}")
    
    # Access Request Evaluation
    print("\n=== Access Request Evaluation ===")
    context = {
        'is_jailbroken': False,
        'location': 'normal_country',
        'access_hour': 14,
        'new_device': False,
        'user_agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'
    }
    result = casb.evaluate_access_request('DEVICE_001', 'user123', 'cloud_storage', context)
    print(f"Access Result: {json.dumps({**result, 'threat_level': result['threat_level'].name}, indent=2)}")
    
    # Generate Report
    print("\n=== Compliance Report ===")
    report = casb.generate_compliance_report()
    print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates CASB functionality: shadow IT detection to discover unauthorized cloud services, DLP (Data Loss Prevention) for pattern/keyword/extension-based content inspection, threat detection by analyzing device posture and access patterns, risk scoring for access decisions, and compliance reporting. This is the security layer between mobile devices and cloud services.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for service discovery and DLP inspection
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for service and log storage
